SEARCH
Package com.mimecast.robin.smtp

Class RefCountedFileMessageSource

java.lang.Object
com.mimecast.robin.smtp.FileMessageSource
com.mimecast.robin.smtp.RefCountedFileMessageSource
All Implemented Interfaces:
MessageSource, Serializable
public class RefCountedFileMessageSource extends FileMessageSource
Reference-counted file message source for safe concurrent access.

This wrapper tracks the number of active consumers of a file-backed message. The backing file is only deleted when all consumers have released their references.

Usage:

  • Call acquire() before sharing with another consumer (e.g., bot threads)
  • Call release() when done processing (e.g., in Session.close())
  • File is automatically deleted when reference count reaches zero

Thread-safe: All reference count operations use atomic operations.

See Also:

    • log

      private static final org.apache.logging.log4j.Logger log

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • refCount

      private transient AtomicInteger refCount
      Reference count tracking active consumers. Starts at 1 for the initial owner.

  • Constructor Details

    • RefCountedFileMessageSource

      public RefCountedFileMessageSource(Path path)
      Constructs a reference-counted file source.
      Parameters:
      path - Message file path.

  • Method Details

    • acquire

      public MessageSource acquire()
      Acquires a reference to this message source.

      Increments the reference count. The caller must ensure a matching release() call when done with the source.

      Returns:
      This message source for chaining.

    • release

      public void release()
      Releases a reference to this message source.

      Decrements the reference count. When the count reaches zero, the backing file is deleted.

    • getRefCount

      public int getRefCount()
      Gets the current reference count.

      Primarily for testing and debugging.

      Returns:
      Current reference count.

    • deleteFile

      private void deleteFile()
      Deletes the backing file.

    • readObject

      private void readObject(ObjectInputStream in) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Custom deserialization to reinitialize transient fields.

      On deserialization, the reference count is reset to 1 since the deserializing consumer becomes the sole owner.

      Throws:
      IOException
      ClassNotFoundException
